For first time VR users, this is a must play!
It gives a short and sweet introduction to the magic of VR.
Unfortunately Meta has failed to update this game for the Quest 3 platform.
Though if you use the Quest Games Optimizer app, the visuals prove to be amazing!
The difference is staggering.

Meta should spend some time updating these older experiences, that are still being used.
Having played this on the Quest 2 on release and then now optimized through QGO on the quest 3 it is safe to say that the game holds up, clearly a lot of love went into it, even though Meta seems to have forgotten about it.

reviews.review_title: incredible game, wish we got more
reviews.review_description:
for starters: i’m new to vr. as in “only played first steps and the beat saber demo” new. first contact was one of the big “shows you what it does” games that i knew about, and i was all for it. little did i know it’d be this good.

yes, it’s simple. you boot up a robot and he gives you floppy discs and what look like NES carts. you find one you like, and you create butterflies, or rockets, or a gun, whatever you want from the choices. it’s not as overbearing as first steps(you’ll be shown what to do, but you gotta learn the rest), yet still a good introduction to vr in general. my only issue with this and some of the other oculus-published games is that they’re too short. i need more, more content, more excuses to play with the robot instead of “look, it’s a demo!”

in conclusion, play it ‘cause it’s awesome, but don’t expect too much.
Skibidy toilet

I'm honestly shocked to bits that this demo environment originally featured for the PC Oculus Rift is not the first experience for players to try and get used to the controls. It's even more mind boggling that it has been tucked away and hidden in the app lab for so long. It's the perfect welcoming app for all ages in a casual and small space. If you're looking to sell family or friends on VR, it's a great place to start!

Hey you! Yeah you! Are you looking for a VR headset or have a VR headset? Well you are in luck! The demo is a little quick gameplay and you don’t need to worry about walking around. This game shows how VR is really like. Even though you just got your VR headset and you are looking for good games. I recommend not hard core games like job simulator. And if you want to go hard core get like Beat Saber or even Gorilla Tag. If you want a multiplayer experience get Rec Room, VR Chat, Gorilla Tag and so much more! So yeah good game for first time in VR!

Listen, it’s cool and all, but I’m going to explain something that happened.

For some context, I’m playing on the Meta Quest 2.

I played it the first time, and was really confused at the ending being so abrupt (Fade to black, kicked to the home screen). So what I did was go back into the game to make sense of things, but realized I probably couldn’t with the small play space I had. I tried to pause the game using the Oculus button on the right controller and it did nothing. I tried several times and used several other buttons but nothing paused the game. I took off the headset in hopes that would do it (I was moving to a different play area so I could move around more anyway), and when I put the headset back on it showed me a previous boundary in that same spot, but I didn’t have my pause menu. I pressed the Oculus button and it didn’t appear. My controllers weren’t showing up either. I turned off the headset and turned it on again to fix the issue. I go back to open the game, and it crashes. I try again and it crashes again, only this time my controllers were stuck in the air in front of me (like a moving screen that follows where you look). I try to turn off the Oculus again and it won’t turn off. I’m holding the power button and nothing happens. I spam click it, I hold it for a few more seconds and the screen goes black, then the prompt to turn off the device shows up for a split second before (without input) it shuts itself off. After about a minute, I turn it back on and uninstall the game. None of this happened before I played this game. I recommend that (if you want to play something like this) that you play “First Steps” (ya know, the learning guide) or “First Hand” (which is this, but a lot more fun and a lot more content). 

TL;DR: I played this game and it almost broke my Oculus.

The original First Steps tutorial is so much better than this, it defies logic why Oculus would make a new one. It's ugly, it's uninteresting, there's none of the sense of fun or wonder that the original First Steps tutorial provides. It takes place in a dark dingy ugly messy cyberpunk style room that no one would ever want to look at. You can literally go anywhere in VR and this takes place in a junk pile? Not the kind of thing you want to show your non-gamer friends if you want them to have any interest in VR.
If you apply the stationary mode it will spawn you either inside or below the desk. Even when you quit out of the app it will still move your spawn point.

Great experience! Besides the normal flow of it, try to think and act out of the box, literally :) Try going behind the walls of the camper, looking behind the corner where the robot had escaped, or finding an Easter Egg on one of the sides of the furniture :) Bet will be fun.

reviews.review_title: Lovely - but far too short an experience
reviews.review_description: I downloaded this without reading anything about it here on the R&R / Info page.   The experience was top-shelf, the interface was perfect.  This is a fantastic "VR Tutorial" for anyone who has never put a VR headset on their head before.    This has the potential to expand into something far longer and more immersive -  After 10 minutes, it just ends.   A GenX'er, I was saddended that this bit of nostalgia ended.  I suppose i'll remain relegated to EchoVR where the 9 year olds yell at you incessantly (until that app dies in August).
Great for learning and good intro into vr

A great way to get into the swing of vr things. Having a little floating Wall-E observing what you do, handing you cartridges and reacting to what you do is always fun. I could imagine as far as kids are concerned that they could replay this game, I would imaging myself revisiting to re-familiarize myself with VR concepts or holding the hands of my friends as they traverse into the vr-scape

reviews.review_title: The great way to start vr!
reviews.review_description: Great way to start vr! Good amount of things to do, explore and more! Recomended mostly for new vr users.

It is a great game but it kicked me out about 10 mins in when I put the golden disc in and it restarted me. I don't know if I beat the game or not.

So much detail in this little scene, and that robots eye contact and shy personality were amazing!  Though, like others have said, the game makes you 3 feet tall if you're sitting down, and I played it through my first time feeling like a five year old trying to peak over a counter top; an awkward way to play of you can't stand up... But kind of doable.  Overall, I love it!

I tried this intro to vr app just out of curiosity after having having my headset for a few days. I was blown away by the realism of the environment in comparison to the default First Steps app. It was cute and had a lot more personality (loved those pink butterflies and the robot). I recommend any beginner give it a look-see.
